_comment: Curated equivalence map for check-ui-string-parity.py: iOS catalogue key
-> Android string name, for labels that must read the SAME to the user but
are (or were) worded differently on the two platforms. A pair whose English
texts already match is silent; a pair that differs is reported as DRIFT to
be aligned (normally by changing the iOS key to Android's wording).
This map is hand-maintained: semantic equivalence cannot be guessed. Add a
pair when a new iOS label corresponds to an Android string under a different
name; the tool's UNMAPPED list flags iOS labels not yet considered.
Seeded in the 0.83.0 UI-parity pass from the Statistics screen, where the
two vocabularies diverged most (Android 'Average per Day' vs iOS 'Per day',
etc.). Entries here are the ALIGNMENT WORK LIST, not yet the aligned state.
